attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Place a chilled head of lettuce on a table.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene is set in a kitchen environment with a tiled floor and a central table. A black chair is positioned under the table. The lighting casts a distinct pattern of bright, diamond-shaped spots across the table and floor, suggesting a light source with a grid or lattice pattern.

**Objects and their types:**

- **Receptacles:**
    - A black **fridge** is visible to the left side of the scene. Its door is closed. It is an openable receptacle.
    - A black **microwave** is on the table, positioned near the back. It is an openable receptacle.
    - The **table** itself acts as a surface for placing objects. While not explicitly defined as a receptacle in the domain, it holds multiple items and serves as a location for interaction.
    - A **sink** is not visible in the provided images.

- **Objects:**
    - There are two distinct **heads of lettuce** on the table. One is on the left side, and the other is on the right. Both are green and appear whole and unsliced.
    - A **knife** is on the table, near the top left, next to the left head of lettuce.
    - A **spoon** is on the table, near the center.
    - A **wine glass** is on the table, near the microwave.
    - A **mug** is on the table, placed on a small black tray next to the microwave.
    - Two **salt shakers** (or similar condiment shakers) are on the table, one near the left lettuce and another near the center.
    - A small, rectangular **green book** is on the table, near the left salt shaker.
    - A **metallic container** with a spherical top is on the table, near the right lettuce.

**Quantities:**

- Receptacles: 2 (fridge, microwave)
- Lettuce: 2
- Knife: 1
- Spoon: 1
- Wine glass: 1
- Mug: 1
- Salt shakers: 2
- Book: 1
- Metallic container: 1

**Spatial Relationships:**

- The **fridge** is located to the left of the table. The shadow from the light source falls on the floor in front of the fridge.
- The **microwave** is on the table, near the back edge, with the mug on its tray and the wine glass next to it.
- The **left head of lettuce** is on the left side of the table, near the knife and one salt shaker.
- The **right head of lettuce** is on the right side of the table, near the metallic container and the spoon.
- The **spoon** is between the two heads of lettuce, slightly closer to the right one.
- The **green book** is to the right of the left salt shaker and to the left of the left head of lettuce.
- The **chair** is positioned under the table, with its legs visible.

**Current State of Objects (relevant to the task):**

- Neither head of lettuce is currently chilled (isCool is false for both).
- Neither head of lettuce is sliced (isSliced is false for both).
- The fridge is closed (opened is false).
- The microwave is closed (opened is false).
- No object is currently held by the agent (holdsAny is false).
- The agent is not at any specific location (atLocation is false for all objects).

**Visual Properties:**

- The **lettuce** heads are bright green with visible leafy textures.
- The **knife** has a metallic blade and a dark handle.
- The **spoon** is metallic and reflective.
- The **wine glass** is clear and empty.
- The **mug** is brown and sits on a black, circular tray.
- The **salt shakers** are transparent with metallic tops.
- The **book** is green with a yellow spine.
- The **metallic container** is cylindrical with a shiny, reflective surface and a spherical top.
- The **table** is light gray with a smooth surface.
- The **fridge** is black with a textured surface and a vertical handle.
- The **microwave** is black and boxy.
- The **chair** is black with a simple, sturdy design.
- The **floor** is made of large, square, beige tiles with visible grout lines.

The task requires placing a chilled head of lettuce on the table. Currently, both heads of lettuce are on the table and are not chilled. The fridge is available for chilling, but it is currently closed. The robot must first go to the fridge, open it, pick up a head of lettuce, place it in the fridge to chill it, and then retrieve it and place it back on the table.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem chill_lettuce)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        fridge1 - fridge
        microwave1 - microwave
        sink1 - sink
        lettuce1 lettuce2 - object
        knife1 - knife
        spoon1 - object
        wineglass1 - object
        mug1 - object
        saltshaker1 saltshaker2 - object
        book1 - object
        container1 - object
        table1 - object
        chair1 - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable fridge1)
        (openable microwave1)
        (not (opened fridge1))
        (not (opened microwave1))
        (not (isCool lettuce1))
        (not (isCool lettuce2))
        (not (isSliced lettuce1))
        (not (isSliced lettuce2))
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (inReceptacle lettuce1 table1)
        (inReceptacle lettuce2 table1)
        (inReceptacle knife1 table1)
        (inReceptacle spoon1 table1)
        (inReceptacle wineglass1 table1)
        (inReceptacle mug1 table1)
        (inReceptacle saltshaker1 table1)
        (inReceptacle saltshaker2 table1)
        (inReceptacle book1 table1)
        (inReceptacle container1 table1)
        (inReceptacle chair1 table1)
        (not (atLocation agent1 fridge1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 microwave1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 sink1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 lettuce1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 lettuce2))
        (not (atLocation agent1 knife1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 spoon1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 wineglass1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 mug1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 saltshaker1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 saltshaker2))
        (not (atLocation agent1 book1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 container1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 table1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 chair1))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle lettuce1 table1)
        (isCool lettuce1)
    ))
)
```
